highlights.message: fix(normalize): recover ×-as-3 in body prose / flattened captions (W0k) + v2.4.112
highlights.body:
The independent Sonnet canary audit found efendic_2022_affect's interaction terms
rendering with a corrupted '3' for '×' in body prose + a flattened italic caption
run -- the table-cell-scoped W0i (v2.4.103) never saw these channels (the third
glyph-fixes-need-all-three-text-channels gap on efendic, after 2-for-minus W0j).
  "the three-way interaction (Direction 3 Manipulated Attribute 3 CMA)"
  "*... Direction 3 manipulated attribute PMA 3direction PMA 3 manipulated ...*"

Fix: recover_times_interaction_glyph_in_prose. The '3' is the single most
dangerous prose glyph, so W0k fires ONLY under a tight signature (ALL hold): line
has "interaction" OR >=2 `Word 3 Word` pairs; the 3 is not preceded/left-flanked
by a reference word (Table/Model/Study/Wave/...); the right flank is not a plural
COUNT noun (3 studies/3 groups); >=1 flank is Title-Case or an all-caps acronym
(predictor name Direction/CMA/PMA). A 2-pass relaxation recovers a
lowercase-lowercase pair inside a line that already resolved a '×' (a confirmed
interaction-term run -- the flattened caption). Wired channel 1 + channel 3.

Verification (AI-gold via article-finder): efendic recovers 13 interaction-term '×'
across the captions + body parenthetical + single Title-Case pairs; idempotent. A
21-case FP battery (Table 3 / Study 3 / osf.io/pg3ae / "ran 3 studies with 3
conditions" / "interaction between age and 3 groups" / "participants (n=3) in 2
waves" / "top 3 items on 5 scales") + a 25-paper wide render scan + the
whole-corpus normalize-IDEMPOTENCY gate all clean. The idempotency gate caught a
case a single-pass battery missed: a reference word as the pair's LEFT FLANK
("Model 3 Relationship") -- a later reflow step can move it into a 2-pair line, so
pass-2 fired; fixed by checking the reference-word set against the left flank.
6 new tests (21 cases). Full suite: 1876 passed, only the 2 documented pre-existing
whitespace_cells failures remain.

Two residual efendic cases queued (harder): a line-WRAPPED interaction term and
the 2x2x3 design-notation shape. See

highlights.message: fix(render): demote abstract-zone Key-Points finding sentence to body (D3) + v2.4.111
highlights.body:
Fixes a PRE-EXISTING failure (test_d3_abstract_zone_no_intermediate_h2, red at
HEAD, not introduced by any recent change -- surfaced when this run's full pytest
ran the whole suite). jama_open_1 rendered "## TRE was more effective for weight
loss" -- a Key-Points FINDING that column-interleaved into the abstract and got
promoted to ##, leaving a stray h2 between ## Abstract and ## Introduction.
_demote_abstract_zone_inline_labels only demoted an explicit allowlist of
structured-abstract LABELS (IMPORTANCE/RESULTS/...), so a finding SENTENCE slipped.

Fix: _is_abstract_finding_sentence. In the abstract zone only, a ## whose text is
a full CLAUSE with a finite VERB (was/reduced/showed/did/...) is demoted to body.
The finite-verb requirement is the discriminator: it demotes "TRE WAS more
effective for weight loss" but keeps a noun-phrase heading like "Effects of Diet
on Body Weight" / "Comparison of TRE and CR" (lowercase prepositions only, no
verb) -- the exact FP class an "any lowercase word" test would have hit. Scoped to
between ## Abstract and the next body-section h2, so blast radius is tiny.

Verification: jama_open_1's finding renders as body; ## Introduction is now the
first h2 after ## Abstract. All 5 test_jama_open_cluster checks pass (D3 was the
only red one). Abstract-zone FP-scan across the rendered corpus demotes ONLY the
jama_open_1 target. 3 new unit-contract tests lock the finding-vs-noun-phrase
discrimination. Full suite: 1855 passed; pre-existing failures down from 3 to 2
(only the whitespace_cells 0-cell regression remains -- diagnosed + queued as its
own investigation: _region_for_caption returns a prose-contaminated region, either
a region-detect regression or a stale test expectation).

highlights.message: fix(render): demote over-promoted Results subsection to ### (C3) + v2.4.110
highlights.body:
The 2026-07-03 canary AI-verify found ip_feldman_2025_pspb rendering
"## Prevalence Estimates Associations with WellBeing (Replication)" as a
top-level ## when the gold has it as a ### Results subsection, sibling to
"### Prevalence Estimate Errors" and "### Intensity Estimate Errors". It is 7
words -- above the <=6-word ### promoter -- so the 5-12-word ## major-section
promoter grabbed it one level too high.

Fix: _demote_parallel_prefix_subsection_headings demotes a ## back to ### ONLY
when a ### heading earlier in the same section (not crossing a different ##
body-section boundary) shares a >=2-word stemmed prefix AND then diverges. Keyed
on PARALLEL PREFIX STRUCTURE, not "nearest ## parent is Results" -- the latter
would wrongly demote the "## Discussion" that also has Results as its nearest ##
above; Discussion shares no prefix with any ### sibling so it is never touched. A
divergence guard stops a ### that is identical to / a pure prefix of the ## title
(a duplicate-heading artifact) from demoting the gold-correct ## (collabra.90203
renders both the correct "## Extension: Perceived Impact of Donation" and a
spurious "### Extension:..." duplicate; the ## is left alone).

Verification (AI-gold via article-finder): ip_feldman's subsection now renders ###
matching gold; its ### siblings and the following ## Discussion unchanged. With
this, ip_feldman's ENTIRE heading cluster matches gold (C1 fixed 5 wrapped
headings v2.4.108, C3 fixes the 1 over-promotion) -- only Table 10 (RC-T) remains.
FP-validated across ~35 rendered corpus papers (20 canary/broad-read + 15 fresh
psychology): the ONLY demotion is the ip_feldman target; collabra's duplicate ##
correctly preserved. HEAD-vs-fix diff: only ip_feldman changes (17 byte-identical).
5 new tests fail at HEAD, pass with fix. Full suite: 1851 passed, only the 3
documented pre-existing failures remain (jama D3 + whitespace_cells x2 -- NOT this
change). WellBeing->Well-Being hyphen in this heading is OCR-tier won't-fix.

highlights.message: fix(normalize): recover 2-for-minus in body PROSE (W0j) + v2.4.109
highlights.body:
An independent Sonnet canary AI-verify found efendic_2022_affect still FAIL: the
A1/A2 table-cell glyph fixes (v2.4.102/103) recovered 2-for-minus and x-as-3
INSIDE <td> cells only, leaving the body-prose channel corrupt
(glyph-fixes-need-all-three-text-channels). Two prose shapes carry a 2-for-minus
glyph with NO bracket CI, so W0b/W0d (which pair a value against its CI) could not
reach them:
  - contrast-coding notes: "direction: 20.5 = low, + 0.5 = high"  (should be -0.5)
  - change M-statistics:   "Mchange = 20.14"  (should be -0.14)

Fix: recover_prose_two_for_minus, keyed on two tight structural signatures, NOT
magnitude:
  - coding note fires ONLY when the "+ X.X = <word>" contrast twin is on the same
    line (a +-k contrast code names both signs; a bare "20.5 = X" is left alone).
  - M-statistic fires ONLY when the M carries a difference-type subscript
    (change/diff/difference/posterior/delta/gain/shift) -- a corrupted "-0.14" and
    a genuine mean age "20.14" have identical digits, so a bare M / Mage is NEVER
    flipped.
Wired into channel 1 (normalize_text, W0j after W0d) AND channel 3
(render post-process) per the 3-channel glyph discipline. NORMALIZATION_VERSION
-> 1.9.40.

Verification (AI-gold via article-finder): efendic's 3 coding notes + 3 Mchange
stats now render with a minus matching gold; 0 residual corruption; idempotent
across 18 papers. FP-validated: a 6-case battery (mean age M=20.14, 20.5% women,
"1 = control, 2 = treatment", genuine M=2.84) changes nothing; a HEAD-vs-fix diff
across 18 rendered corpus papers changes ONLY efendic (17 byte-identical). 5 new
tests fail at HEAD, pass with fix. Full suite: 1848 passed, only the 3 documented
pre-existing failures remain (jama D3 abstract-zone + whitespace_cells x2 -- NOT
introduced by this change).

The remaining efendic findings (x-as-3 in prose/caption -- harder, needs
Title-Case-flank signature; Sage masthead + running-header leak; two demoted
subsection headings) are queued in docs/FINDINGS_2026-07-03_efendic_glyph_in_prose_channel.md.

highlights.message: fix(render): rejoin wrapped Results-subsection headings into one ### (C1) + v2.4.108
highlights.body:
ip_feldman_2025_pspb column-wrapped FIVE long Results/Discussion-subsection
headings across 2-3 physical lines with no blank between head/tail/body. The
downstream promoters mangled the same paper three ways: the <=6-word ### promoter
fired on the head alone and stranded the wrapped tail in body; the 5-12-word ##
major promoter grabbed a stranded tail as its own over-promoted heading; or the
whole title fell to body. Gold has each as ONE ### heading.

Fix: _rejoin_wrapped_subsection_heading runs FIRST among the isolated-heading
promoters, rejoins the wrap into one line, and emits it already promoted to ###.
Strict structural signature (no paper identity): paragraph-isolated Title-Case
head at a clean sentence boundary, GLUED to 1-2 heading-ish tail lines, over
genuine body prose; head must hang (continuation word / possessive / colon-title)
or tail ends in a balanced (qualifier); head substantial (colon or >=3 words) so a
2-word figure-diagram node-label pair ("Support for"+"Leader") is never merged;
not figure-caption-adjacent; joined title <=115 chars / <=14 words.

Verification (AI-gold via article-finder, never pdftotext): all 5 ip_feldman
wrapped headings now single ### matching gold; independent Sonnet canary verify
confirms the wrapped-heading class is resolved. Generalizes: also reunites
collabra.90203 "Associations between Aggregated Feelings and Hypothetical
Donations" (gold-confirmed) and psych-sci "Contextual Confidence and Postdecision
Processing" (both were plain body = SECTION-BOUNDARY defect). 16-paper FP scan =
0 prose-merges; 18-paper HEAD-vs-fix content-loss scan = 0 words lost, only the 3
papers with genuine wrapped headings changed (other 15 byte-identical). 5 new
tests fail at HEAD, pass with fix.

Also fixes test-consistency debt from the v2.4.105 SECTIONING_VERSION 1.2.2->1.2.3
bump (left red until this run's full-suite run surfaced it): 2 stale unit
assertions + 3 golden snapshots expected 1.2.2; the CLI test now asserts against
the imported SECTIONING_VERSION so it won't go stale on future bumps; goldens
regenerated (version field only, no content drift). No library code changed for
that part.

highlights.message: fix(render): strip body author-affiliation footnote block (efendic) + v2.4.107
highlights.body:
The cycle-1 canary AI-verify found efendic_2022_affect leaking its author
affiliation + corresponding-author block into the MIDDLE of an Introduction
paragraph. The block is a page-1 bottom footnote that pdftotext serialised in
reading order, splitting a sentence:
  "…can be far easier--more" | <Maastricht University … Contributed equally,
   joint first authors … Gilad Feldman, … Pok Fo Lam Road …> |
  "efficient--than weighing…"
The front-matter masthead strip only covers the H1->first-## zone, so this
body-position block was missed.

Fix: _strip_body_affiliation_block removes a RUN of >=3 consecutive
affiliation-shaped lines beginning after the front-matter, reconnecting the
split paragraph. Block-level and strict, so a lone body university mention is
never touched.

CRITICAL guard found via a logged corpus content-loss scan: the first cut passed
all unit FP tests AND 387 render/masthead/sections tests, but a scan that LOGS
the exact lines each paper strips revealed it was removing REFERENCE-LIST blocks
on 5 of 6 firing papers (a bibliography is a run of "Author, X. Y. (YEAR). …
University Press …" lines that superficially match affiliation grammar). Fixed
with a citation-line guard (parenthesised publication year OR author-initials
opener). Re-scan: 1/20 papers stripped, both genuine affiliation blocks — the
fix even GENERALIZES to jesp.2012.11.004's affiliation leak. Added a
test_leaves_reference_list_block regression.

Bump: __version__/pyproject 2.4.107 (render-level change).

Verification (ground truth = AI multimodal read via article-finder reading golds,
never pdftotext): efendic — the affiliation block is gone from the Introduction
and the split sentence reconnects; the legitimate author-bio university mention
later in the paper is preserved. Independent Sonnet AI-gold re-verify: efendic
now PASS (full-document scan found NO remaining glyph, ×, SE-sign, table, heading,
or metadata-leak defect). 387 + 136 render/masthead/reference tests pass (incl. 5
new affiliation tests); logged corpus content-loss scan (20 papers) confirms the
strip removes only affiliation footnotes, never body content.

Milestone: efendic is the run's first fully-resolved canary — its glyph cluster
(A1 2-for-minus, A2 ×-for-3, A3 SE-sign) plus this affiliation leak are all fixed.
Remaining canary defects live on other papers (ip_feldman C1/C3 + Table 10;
plos_med/chandrashekar RC-T table-loss; ar_apa figure-leak + OCR-tier .245).
Standing corpus verdict: FAIL.

highlights.message: fix(title): drop Elsevier CrossMark "T" widget from the H1 title + v2.4.106
highlights.body:
A cycle-5 broad-read found multiple Elsevier / ScienceDirect (JESP) papers
rendering a stray uppercase "T" INSIDE the H1 title:
  "Choosing persuasion targets: How expectations of qualitative change T
   increase advocacy intentions"
  "Children's and adults' understanding of punishment and the criminal
   justice T system"

Root cause: the CrossMark "Check for updates" widget renders as a lone
"T"-shaped glyph positioned in the title y-band (right edge, in a slightly
smaller font — height 10.0 vs the title's 13.4), so _compute_layout_title picks
it up as a same-y-band title word and merges it mid-run between the two wrapped
title lines.

Fix: _compute_layout_title drops a bare single-character "T" title word. A real
title never contains a standalone uppercase "T" as its own word, so this is safe
and general — it corrects every Elsevier / ScienceDirect paper carrying the
CrossMark widget, not just the two the broad-read happened to sample.

Bump: __version__/pyproject 2.4.106 (render-level change; no NORMALIZATION /
SECTIONING version change).

Verification (ground truth = AI multimodal read via article-finder reading golds,
never pdftotext): both JESP titles render clean ("…qualitative change increase
advocacy intentions", "…criminal justice system"); the canary titles (ip_feldman,
efendic, chandrashekar) are unaffected. 320 render + title + masthead +
normalization tests pass (incl. 2 new real-PDF CrossMark tests); a corpus title
scan across 16 Elsevier/Sage papers = 0 titles with a stray " T " (only the
CrossMark artifact removed, no real content lost).

This ships a corpus-wide Elsevier title fix (E1). It targets non-canary papers;
the canary corpus is still 5/5 FAIL (efendic affiliation-leak, ip_feldman
C1/C3/Table10, plos_med/chandrashekar RC-T table-loss, ar_apa figure-leak),
queued. Standing verdict: FAIL.

highlights.message: fix(sections): promote Sage/APA back-matter headings via taxonomy + v2.4.105
highlights.body:
The cycle-1 canary AI-verify found ip_feldman_2025_pspb rendering three
back-matter section headings as plain body text while their siblings
(Acknowledgments, Author Contributions, Funding) rendered as ##:
"Authorship Declaration", "Declaration of Conflicting Interests", "ORCID iDs".

Root cause: in Sage PSPB back-matter typography these three immediately precede
their paragraph with NO blank line, so only canonical-heading recognition (not
the line-isolated fallback, which needs the heading alone on its line) can
promote them — and these exact wordings were missing from
docpluck/sections/taxonomy.py (lookup_canonical_label returned None for all
three). Because the heading-detection alternation is built directly from the
taxonomy dict, the fix is a one-point addition:
  - "declaration of conflicting interests"/"...interest" -> conflict_of_interest
    (the Sage wording, distinct from the "declaration of competing interest"
    already covered)
  - "authorship declaration"/"authorship statement" -> author_contributions
  - "orcid ids" -> author_note

Only the PLURAL "orcid ids" heading form is canonical: a bare/singular/inline
"ORCID:" is deliberately excluded so a line-leading identifier ("ORCID: 0000-…")
is never false-promoted, and the inline "(ORCID: …)" mentions inside the
Acknowledgments paragraph stay body text.

Bumps: __version__/pyproject 2.4.105, SECTIONING_VERSION 1.2.3.

Verification (ground truth = AI multimodal read via article-finder reading golds,
never pdftotext): ip_feldman render — all three headings now ## ; inline
Acknowledgments ORCID identifier preserved as body. Independent Sonnet AI-gold
re-verify: C2 resolved, no collateral regression outside back-matter, siblings
intact. 359 sections + heading + taxonomy tests pass (incl. 3 new taxonomy
tests + 2 real-PDF ip_feldman tests). Adversarial FP battery: 4 body sentences
containing the phrases mid-sentence -> 0 canonical-heading promotions
(lookup requires an exact normalized match, so a long body sentence never
normalizes to the short heading string — inherently FP-safe).

This ships ONE root-cause class (C2 back-matter demotion); ip_feldman still FAILs
on C1 (wrapped-heading split), C3 (Prevalence heading over-promoted + WellBeing
hyphen drop), and Table 10 empty, queued as next cycles. Standing verdict: FAIL.

highlights.message: fix(glyph): stop dropped-minus recovery sign-flipping SE table cells (efendic) + v2.4.104
highlights.body:
The third efendic_2022_affect defect from the cycle-1 canary sweep: every
regression table's standard-error (SE) column rendered some values negative
(Intercept SE = 0.06 -> -0.06), impossible because a standard error is
non-negative.

Root cause (reproduce-before-fix corrected a wrong first hypothesis). The guess
was W0d over-recovering a corrupt 2X.XX SE cell; instrumenting the actual render
path disproved it — the SE cell arrives genuinely 0.06, W0d leaves it, and the
minus is added later by recover_dropped_minus_via_ci_pairing (the DROPPED-minus
recovery). In the "B | SE | CI" row the CI describes the B estimate; once B is
recovered to -0.09, the recovery finds the SE 0.06 (whose negation -0.06 fits
B's CI [-0.21, 0.04] while 0.06 does not) and flips it. But the SE legitimately
falls outside the estimate's CI — it is a different column from the estimate the
CI pairs with.

Fix (A3 guard): in a <td> row, do not flip a bare-positive token when a
signed-negative number (the already-recovered estimate) precedes it — that
earlier negative is the CI's estimate, so this token is a later variance column
(SE/SD), non-negative, and must not be flipped. Scoped to <td> rows via the
B->SE->CI column-order invariant; a prose line keeps the original behavior so a
genuinely-first dropped-minus estimate still recovers (ar_apa body betas
byte-identical). The guard only ever BLOCKS a flip (strictly more conservative),
so it cannot introduce a new sign error.

Bumps: __version__/pyproject 2.4.104, NORMALIZATION_VERSION 1.9.39.

Verification (ground truth = AI multimodal read via article-finder reading golds,
never pdftotext): efendic Camelot-ON — every SE value renders positive (Intercept
0.06/0.10/0.15 matching gold); genuine negative B-coefficients + × interaction
terms (A2) unchanged; independent Sonnet AI-gold re-verify: A3 resolved, 0 new
regressions, affiliation-leak now the ONLY remaining efendic defect. 109
dropped-minus + minus-recovery + idempotence tests pass (incl 4 new A3 tests); 335
broad render/sections/normalization pass; a corpus diff of old-vs-new recovery
over 14 papers' table rows = 0 rows changed (guard blocks only SE over-recoveries).

Milestone: efendic's GLYPH cluster (A1 2-for-minus, A2 ×-for-3, A3 SE-sign) is
complete. Only the mid-Introduction affiliation leak (a METADATA-LEAK, different
layer) remains. Standing corpus verdict: FAIL.

highlights.message: fix(glyph): recover ×-as-3 in interaction-term table cells (efendic) + v2.4.103
highlights.body:
The cycle-1 AI-gold canary re-verify surfaced a second glyph corruption on efendic_2022_affect: the multiplication sign × (interaction operator) decodes as the literal digit 3 in every predictor name across regression Tables 2-5 — "Direction × manipulated attribute" reads "Direction 3 manipulated attribute", "Pleasure × Arousal × CMA" reads "Pleasure 3 Arousal 3 CMA". Same broken-ToUnicode AdvPS… subset font that maps U+2212 minus to '2' (v2.4.102) and '<' to '\' (v2.4.39). recover_times_interaction_glyph (W0i) is TABLE-CELL SCOPED — wired only into cell_cleaning._html_escape, NEVER normalize_text or the render post-process — because a bare '3' between letters is ambiguous in prose ("Table 3 summarizes", "osf.io/pg3ae") but unambiguous in a Camelot predictor cell. Guards: a reference word before the 3 (Model/Study/Wave/…) keeps a genuine ordinal; the cell must carry a ≥3-letter word; recovery reaches across a wrap break (
/merge placeholder) so a 3-way interaction wrapped mid-cell recovers both operators. CRITICAL guard added after a canary-coverage catch: the first cut converted maier_2023_collabra's hypothesis labels H3a/H3b -> "H × a"/"H × b" (a false positive a 12/18-paper random FP scan had missed). iterate-gate.sh --cycle 2 FAILED on I2 (canary-coverage), forcing a maier render that surfaced it. Fix: require whitespace (or a wrap-break) on at least one side of the 3 — interaction terms have a space; glued single-letter labels (H3a, M3, x3y) do not, so they are excluded. Lesson recorded: a sampled corpus scan is necessary but NOT sufficient; the fixed canary roster is the backstop (project lesson + LEARNINGS). Bumps: __version__/pyproject 2.4.103, NORMALIZATION_VERSION 1.9.38. Verification (ground truth = AI multimodal read via article-finder reading golds, never pdftotext): efendic Camelot-ON 22->0 corrupt interaction cells (16 × recovered); genuine Table 3 / Figure 3 refs preserved; maier H3a/H3b + genuine factorial "× 3 (" notation untouched; fixed-3 (ip_feldman/plos_med/chandrashekar) BYTE-IDENTICAL to their pre-A2 renders; independent Sonnet AI-gold re-verify: A2 resolved, 0 new regressions, B-column minus (A1) still correct. Whole-corpus FP scan ~27,000 Camelot cells across ~30 papers (two seeds) = 0 cells changed. A2 unit + real-PDF suite 12 pass; broad render/sections/normalization 385 pass. This ships ONE root-cause class (A2); efendic still FAILs on A3 (SE-column spurious minus) + an affiliation leak, queued as next cycles. Standing corpus verdict: FAIL. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8

highlights.message: fix(glyph): recover 2-for-minus in Camelot HTML table cells (efendic B-column) + v2.4.102
highlights.body:
W0d recover_minus_via_ci_pairing paired a bracket-less point estimate with the
CI in its same record and recovered the '2'-for-U+2212 minus by the containment
invariant — but Camelot emits each <td> on its own line, so the SE cell between
the B-column estimate and the CI cell pushed the char-gap past the 30-char
bare-bracket proximity cap. The recovery fired in the DISABLE_CAMELOT
unstructured-table channel (CI on the same text line) but silently missed every
negative B-coefficient in the Camelot HTML-table channel — the production
default. efendic Tables 2-5: 27 corrupt cells (`20.09` for `-0.09`), a
silently-wrong statistic. The existing test passed only because it set
DOCPLUCK_DISABLE_CAMELOT=1 (a channel blind spot).

Fix: inside an HTML <tr> columns pair by table geometry, not prose adjacency, so
a bare bracket now uses the relaxed (labeled) proximity, still guarded by
_INDEPENDENT_STAT_BETWEEN_RE (rejects pairing across a different estimate's
column). A prose line (no <td>) keeps the strict 30-char cap. Keyed on <tr>
structure, not paper identity -> general. 27->0 corrupt cells; genuine 2.56
(Direction row, in [2.42,2.69]) preserved by containment; idempotent.

Also (pre-existing, leave-nothing-behind): the independent-stat guard now runs
for EVERY bracket kind, not just labeled. A tight-spaced prose variant
`M=5.37, SD=2.01, t(1827)=1.83, d=0.09 [-1.86,0.04]` is 25 chars from 2.01 to
the bracket (within the cap), so the gap check alone let SD=2.01 wrongly recover
to -.01; the CI is d's and t/d intervene -> now rejected.

Bumps: __version__/pyproject 2.4.102, NORMALIZATION_VERSION 1.9.37.

Verification (ground truth = AI multimodal read via article-finder reading golds,
never pdftotext): efendic Camelot-ON 27->0 corrupt B-cells; independent Sonnet
AI-gold re-verify confirms A1 resolved, B-column exact, 0 new regressions. Full
minus suite 22 pass (6 new); adversarial over-recovery battery all pass; broad
normalize/render/sections/heading 557 pass; flatten 39; RC-T degenerate-table
(Camelot) 8; deterministic DISABLE_CAMELOT body-text diff of 8 corpus papers = 0
changed (surgical).

Cycle-1 canary baseline at v2.4.101 was 5/5 FAIL (fresh TRIAGE
docs/TRIAGE_2026-07-03_head_v2.4.101_assessment.md). This ships ONE root-cause
class (A1); efendic still FAILs on A2 (x-for-3), A3 (SE-spurious-minus), and an
affiliation leak, queued as next cycles. Standing corpus verdict: FAIL.

highlights.message: fix(tables): strip LONG caption-tail prose header + version bump v2.4.101
highlights.body:
- _is_caption_tail_prose: recognize a long caption-continuation prose fragment
  (>=8 words, function-word-dense, sentence-ending, no stat) as a leading
  single-cell row, not just the short <=40-char form. Mirrors the phantom-strip's
  body-prose-in-<th> detector so the tail is removed UPSTREAM rather than tripping
  render._strip_phantom_camelot_tables, which drops the ENTIRE table.
  Fixes jdm_.2023.16 Table 7 (14x10 real grid was vanishing at render because its
  absorbed caption-tail header row triggered the phantom-strip).
- window the multi-cell-below context guard (scan next 5 rows, not just the next)

digest.narrative: Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. The `statcheck` and `docpluck` projects have made administrative updates and fixes, including recovering residual prose shapes and stripping orphaned affiliation remnants. Meanwhile, `coarse` has re-enabled Fable 5 in the model picker, and `regcheck` has improved its backend handling and made aesthetic bug fixes. Notably, `escicheck` has synchronized its `main` branch with the latest version of `effectcheck`, and `auto-zcurve` has revised its validation note to include a citation. Additionally, `output-it-forward` has added a convenient Chrome Webstore link for easy access. These updates demonstrate the connections and collaborations within our community, and we celebrate the progress being made across these open-source research tools.
